# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create an Azure ARM template for a Storage Account to hold application data and user uploads. Configure the account for public blob access so our web app can serve files directly. Set AccessTier to Hot for performance. This is for our MVP deployment that needs quick access to stored files.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

$schema: "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#"
contentVersion: "1.0.0.0"
resources:
  - type: "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts"
    apiVersion: "2021-04-01"
    name: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    sku:
      name: "Standard_LRS"
      tier: "Standard"
    kind: "StorageV2"
    properties:
      accessTier: "Hot"
      supportsHttpsTrafficOnly: true
      minimumTlsVersion: "TLS1_2"
      allowBlobPublicAccess: true
    identity:
      type: "SystemAssigned"
    resources:
      - type: "blobServices"
        apiVersion: "2021-04-01"
        name: "default"
        properties:
          cors:
            corsRules: []
parameters:
  storageAccountName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: "The name of the storage account. Must be between 3 and 24 characters in length and use numbers and lower-case letters only."
  location:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: "The location where the storage account will be created."
